data.title: Optery: The Best Way to Remove Your Data Online
data.excerpt: Optery automatically deletes your personal info from data brokers. Protect your identity now. Read this Optery review and start for free today.

$media.alt: Optery: The Best Way to Remove Your Data Online

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*

I spend a lot of time testing software tools, and every once in a while, something genuinely surprises me. Not because it does something flashy, but because it solves a problem I didn't realize was eating at me. That's exactly what happened with Optery.

If you've ever Googled your own name and found your home address, phone number, or family members' info listed on sites you never signed up for — you already know the problem. Data brokers scrape, package, and sell your personal information constantly. And getting them to stop? Manually contacting hundreds of sites, sending opt-out requests, following up when they "forget" to delete you — it's a nightmare nobody has time for.
Optery fixes that. Completely. Automatically. Without you lifting a finger after the initial setup.
Check out Optery and start removing your data today
What Is Optery and How Does It Work?

Optery is an online privacy service that removes your personal information from hundreds of data broker websites on your behalf. Think of it as a full-service data deletion engine. You give it the details you want protected — your name, addresses, phone numbers, email addresses — and it systematically contacts data brokers, submits opt-out requests, verifies removals, and monitors for reappearances.

Here's the part that impressed me most: it doesn't just fire off a bunch of emails and hope for the best. Optery maintains a database of over 300 data broker sites, each with different opt-out procedures, timelines, and quirks. The platform navigates all of that complexity automatically. Some brokers require form submissions. Others need emails. Some have CAPTCHAs or multi-step verification processes. Optery handles every variation without you ever seeing the friction.
When you first sign up, Optery runs an
exposure report

— a scan that shows you exactly where your data currently appears online, complete with screenshots. It's eye-opening. I found my information on sites I'd never heard of, listings that included everything from my date of birth to property records. Seeing it laid out like that made the decision to use Optery feel urgent.

After the initial scan, Optery begins submitting removal requests across its entire broker network. The platform tracks each request, follows up when removals don't happen on schedule, and provides ongoing monitoring so that if your data resurfaces on a new broker — which it often does — Optery catches it and removes it again.
The whole process runs continuously. It's not a one-time cleanup. It's persistent privacy protection.
Key Features and What's New
Optery has been steadily expanding its capabilities, and the current version of the platform is seriously impressive. Here's what stands out:
Exposure Reports with Screenshots

— This is where your Optery journey begins. The platform scans your name across hundreds of data broker sites and produces a detailed report showing exactly where your information appears. Each listing includes a screenshot so you can see the exposed data with your own eyes. It's not abstract — it's concrete proof of how much of your personal life is publicly available.
Coverage of 300+ Data Brokers

— Optery doesn't just hit the big names. It covers a massive range of data brokers, people-search sites, and data aggregators. New brokers are added regularly as the data privacy landscape evolves. The breadth of coverage is one of the reasons I recommend it over trying to do this manually.
Automated Removal and Re-Removal

— Data brokers are notorious for re-listing people after removal. Optery monitors for this and automatically re-submits requests when your data reappears. This is the feature that makes the ongoing subscription worthwhile. Privacy isn't a one-time event — it requires maintenance, and Optery handles that maintenance for you.
Search Engine Scrubbing

— Beyond data brokers, Optery also scans Google and Bing search results for exposed personal information. If your address or phone number shows up in search results linking to broker sites, Optery works to get those results cleaned up too.
Free Basic Monitoring
— You can sign up for free and get a basic exposure report with ongoing monitoring. This lets you see exactly what Optery can find before committing to a paid plan. No credit card required.
For the latest additions to the platform, check out
Optery's newest features
. They've been shipping updates consistently, and each one adds meaningful value.
Who Is Optery Ideal For?
I think about this in terms of real scenarios rather than demographics. Here's who benefits most:
Anyone who's been doxxed or stalked.
If you've experienced harassment where someone used your online data to find you, Optery is a direct solution. It removes the information that bad actors rely on.
Public-facing professionals.

Teachers, journalists, law enforcement officers, healthcare workers, attorneys — anyone whose job puts them in contact with the public. These professionals are disproportionately targeted by data brokers because there's public interest in finding them.
People going through divorce or custody disputes.
This is a use case that doesn't get talked about enough. During high-conflict separations, having your address and personal details readily available online creates genuine safety concerns.
Families with children.

Data brokers don't just list adults. They compile information about minors associated with family records. Parents who care about their children's digital footprint from an early age find Optery essential.
Privacy-conscious individuals who simply value their autonomy.

You don't need a dramatic reason to want your personal information removed from the internet. You might just believe that your data is yours, and companies shouldn't profit from selling it without your explicit consent.
Business owners and executives.

Corporate leaders often have their business and personal information intertwined across broker sites. Removing personal data reduces phishing risk, social engineering attacks, and unwanted solicitation.
If any of these describe you,
Optery is worth a serious look
. It's one of those tools where the cost is trivial compared to the risk it mitigates.
Optery Pricing Breakdown
One thing I appreciate about Optery is its transparent, tiered pricing structure. There's no bait-and-switch, no hidden fees, and a 30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans.
Free Basic Plan ($0)

— This gets you a personalized exposure report with screenshots, ongoing monitoring scans of Google and Bing search results, and self-service opt-out guidance. No credit card required. This is a genuinely useful free tier — not a stripped-down teaser. It lets you see the scope of your exposure and decide if you want the automated removal.
Core-Unlimited Plan

— The mid-tier offering includes everything in the free plan plus fully automated removal from hundreds of data brokers, unlimited removal requests, removal from people-search sites, and priority processing. This is the plan most individuals will want.
Extended Plan

— For users who want maximum coverage, the Extended plan adds removal from additional data sources, family member coverage (up to a specified number), and enhanced monitoring with faster scan frequencies.
Ultimate Plan
— The top tier includes all previous features plus coverage for the widest range of data sources, maximum family members, and the most aggressive monitoring schedule.

All paid plans can be switched from monthly to yearly billing, which saves you 17%. Given that data broker removal is an ongoing process — not a one-and-done deal — the yearly plans offer solid value.
I also have some
practical tips for getting the most out of Optery
that help maximize what you get from whichever plan you choose.
Why Optery Stands Out in Privacy Protection
There are a handful of data removal services on the market now. I've tested several, and here's why Optery consistently earns my recommendation:
Transparency.

The exposure report with actual screenshots sets Optery apart. Most competitors tell you they found your data. Optery shows you. That distinction matters because it builds trust — you can verify the work being done on your behalf.
Breadth of coverage.

Covering 300+ brokers isn't just a number. It means Optery catches listings on niche and regional sites that smaller services miss. The data broker ecosystem is fragmented, and comprehensive coverage is the only way to get meaningful results.
Ongoing automation.
Some services run a one-time removal sweep and call it done. Optery knows that data resurfaces. Its automated re-removal process is what makes it a true privacy service rather than a temporary fix.
The free tier is actually useful.

Many companies offer "free" plans that are so limited they're functionally useless. Optery's free monitoring gives you real value — ongoing scans, exposure reports, search engine checks — before you ever pay a dime.
Continuous improvement.
The Optery team regularly adds new brokers, refines their removal processes, and ships platform updates. The
reasons to choose Optery
keep growing as the product evolves.

I also like that they operate on PartnerStack, which means their affiliate and referral infrastructure is clean and reliable. If you care about supporting creators and reviewers who recommend tools, that ecosystem works well.
My Experience Using Optery
I signed up expecting a decent experience. What I got was a wake-up call.

The exposure report found my personal information on over 40 data broker sites. Home addresses going back fifteen years. Phone numbers I haven't used in a decade. Email addresses tied to old accounts. Names of family members listed as "associates." It was unsettling to see how much of my life was packaged and available for anyone willing to pay a few dollars.

Within the first week of my paid plan, Optery had submitted removal requests to every single one of those sites. By the end of the second week, a significant portion of listings had been confirmed removed. The dashboard made it easy to track progress — each broker showed its status, and I could see screenshots of before and after.

The part I didn't expect was the re-removal. About six weeks in, Optery flagged that two of my previously removed listings had resurfaced. It automatically re-submitted requests. I didn't have to do anything. That's the kind of passive, continuous protection that makes the subscription worth it month after month.
For a detailed walkthrough, I wrote a
step-by-step guide on using Optery
that covers everything from signup to advanced settings.
Common Use Cases for Optery
I've already covered who benefits most, but the specific
use cases for Optery
are worth highlighting because they show the practical value:
Reducing spam calls and junk mail.
When your phone number and mailing address are on fewer broker sites, the volume of unsolicited contact drops noticeably. Multiple users report significant reductions within the first month.
Preventing identity theft.
Data brokers are a primary source of information for identity thieves. The less of your data floating around, the harder it is for someone to impersonate you.
Controlling your digital reputation.
People-search sites often surface outdated or inaccurate information alongside accurate data. Removing those listings gives you more control over what people find when they look you up.
Protecting your physical safety.
For survivors of domestic violence, stalking, or harassment, having your address easily findable online is a legitimate safety risk. Optery addresses this directly.
Simplifying compliance.
For individuals working in regulated industries, minimizing unnecessary data exposure aligns with best practices around information security and privacy.
The Bottom Line: Is Optery Worth It?
Yes. Unequivocally.

If you care about your privacy — and you should, because the data broker industry processes billions of records annually — then a tool like Optery isn't a luxury. It's infrastructure. It's the equivalent of having good locks on your doors.

The free plan lets you see exactly how exposed you are. The paid plans handle the removal automatically and keep monitoring over time. The pricing is fair, the 30-day money-back guarantee removes any risk, and the 17% yearly discount rewards commitment.
Start with Optery for free and see where your data is exposed
. Once you see that exposure report, I'm confident you'll understand why I recommend this tool so strongly. Your personal information is valuable — Optery makes sure you're the one who controls it.

children.text: How long does it take for Optery to remove my data from data brokers?

Most removal requests are processed within 1-3 weeks, though some data brokers take longer to comply. Optery tracks each request and follows up automatically if a broker doesn't respond within their expected timeframe. You can monitor progress in real time through your dashboard.
Is Optery's free plan actually useful, or is it just a sales funnel?

Optery's free plan is genuinely valuable on its own. It includes a personalized exposure report with screenshots, ongoing monitoring scans of Google and Bing search results, and self-service opt-out guidance — all without requiring a credit card. Many users start free and upgrade once they see the full scope of their data exposure.
Can data brokers re-list my information after Optery removes it?

Yes, data brokers frequently re-collect and re-list personal information. This is exactly why Optery offers continuous monitoring and automated re-removal. If your data resurfaces on a previously cleaned site, Optery detects it and resubmits removal requests without any action needed from you.
